note: Gin Toolbar is a passive companion module for the Gin admin theme with no config UI, permissions, plugins, or services. It works purely through theme overrides and library attachments, so all cases are `recipe` (response-graded), not `execution` (live-state).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, what does the gin_toolbar module do and why would I need it alongside the Gin theme? Answer in one or two sentences.
evals.expected_output: Gin Toolbar renders Gin's styled admin toolbar/navigation on front-end (non-admin) pages so logged-in users get the Gin-themed toolbar site-wide, because admin themes cannot control the toolbar on front-end pages.

evals.prompt: I want to install gin_toolbar via Composer on Drupal 11. What is the package name, and does it require any other project to actually work?
evals.expected_output: composer require drupal/gin_toolbar; it requires the Gin theme (drupal/gin) — the toolbar only activates when Gin or a Gin subtheme is the active admin or default theme.

evals.prompt: I enabled gin_toolbar but the styled toolbar is not appearing. Under what condition does gin_toolbar actually activate on a Drupal site?
evals.expected_output: It only activates when Gin (or a Gin subtheme) is set as the active admin or default theme, and the user has the access toolbar / access navigation permission. It is inert otherwise (e.g. for anonymous visitors or non-Gin admin themes).

evals.prompt: I built a subtheme of Gin as my admin theme. Will gin_toolbar still give me the site-wide Gin toolbar, or does it only work with Gin itself?
evals.expected_output: Yes — a Gin subtheme gets the same site-wide toolbar treatment as Gin itself; gin_toolbar activates when Gin or a Gin subtheme is the active admin/default theme.

evals.prompt: Where do I configure gin_toolbar's appearance settings, like accent color, dark mode, or the toolbar variant? Point me to the settings page.
evals.expected_output: Gin Toolbar has no configuration of its own; all appearance options (accent color, focus color, dark mode, high-contrast, toolbar variant) come from the Gin theme's settings and are carried into the front-end toolbar.

evals.prompt: Does gin_toolbar support the classic vertical Gin toolbar and the horizontal toolbar variant on the front end, and where is that choice made?
evals.expected_output: Yes — based on Gin's classic_toolbar setting, gin_toolbar attaches the matching front-end library (classic vertical, horizontal, navigation, or the new toolbar), so the choice is made in the Gin theme settings.

evals.prompt: My Drupal 11 site uses core's Navigation module for the left sidebar. Does gin_toolbar work with that, and what does it do differently?
evals.expected_output: Yes — gin_toolbar integrates with core's Navigation module: it attaches the core_navigation library, skips its own navigation template override when Navigation is enabled, and styles the left sidebar menu with Gin.
